title: quercus falcata subsp. pagodifolia
intro: Quercus falcata subsp. pagodifolia, also known as the southern red oak or shingle oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the southeastern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where the bark and leaves are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits associated with Quercus falcata subsp. pagodifolia include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Herbal preparations often involve the tree's bark and leaves being made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
